changes_made: Changed k8s_apiserver_secure_transport_policy to k8s_apiserver_tls_enforced for consistency and clarity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls related to dynamic information flow control. The naming conventions mostly follow the prescribed pattern, but there was a need to refine the API server function name for consistency with existing patterns. The functions are technically accurate, addressing network policies, ingress TLS, service mesh mTLS, and API server secure transport.
naming_improvements: Use 'tls_enforced' instead of 'secure_transport_policy' for API server to align with existing naming conventions
title: Information Flow Enforcement - Information flow enforcement | dynamic information flow control enforce [ assign
step1_reasoning: The control AC-4(3) focuses on dynamic information flow control, which in a Kubernetes context can be mapped to network policies and secure communication configurations. NetworkPolicies can enforce dynamic flow control by specifying ingress and egress rules. TLS enforcement for Ingress and mTLS in service mesh configurations ensure secure transport. API server secure transport policies ensure encrypted communication with the cluster.
